message:
feat(wizard): add VPS/Local badges and Windows Terminal setup guide

Problem: Users (especially beginners like Steven, 75yo) were confused about
where commands should be run - locally vs on the VPS. Common mistakes:
- Installing Claude Desktop app instead of using Claude Code on VPS
- Running `claude` in PowerShell locally instead of SSHing to VPS first

Solution:
- Add `runLocation` prop to CommandCard showing "Run on VPS" or "Run on your
  computer" badges with visual distinction (cyan for VPS, amber for local)
- Add prominent warnings on status-check page about Claude Desktop confusion
- Add reconnection reminder before all post-install command sections
- Create new Windows Terminal SSH preset guide page for one-click VPS access
- Add navigation links to Windows Terminal guide from key pages

Changes:
- components/command-card.tsx: Add LocationBadge with Server/Monitor icons
- app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx: Add warnings + runLocation to all commands
- app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx: Add runLocation + Windows Terminal link
- app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx: Add runLocation to installer command
- app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx: Add runLocation to exit/SSH commands
- app/wizard/verify-key-connection/page.tsx: Add runLocation + Windows link
- app/wizard/windows-terminal-setup/page.tsx: New step-by-step guide
- lib/analytics.ts: Add windows_terminal_setup to WizardStep type

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -118,6 +118,44 @@ export default function StatusCheckPage() {

+ {/* Reconnection Reminder */} + +
+

+ Make sure you're connected to your VPS, not running commands on your laptop! +

+

+ If you're in PowerShell or Terminal on your laptop, first run your SSH command: +

+ + ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_VPS_IP + +

+ Once you see ubuntu@ in your prompt, you're ready. +

+
+ + + {/* Common Mistake Warning */} + +
+

+ Claude Code is NOT the Claude Desktop app you download to your computer. +

+

+ Claude Code is a command-line tool that's already installed on your VPS. + To use it: +

+
    +
  1. SSH into your VPS first (using the command above)
    2. +
  2. Then run claude or cc commands
    3. +
+

+ If you're seeing "command not found" in PowerShell or Terminal on your laptop, you're in the wrong place! +

+
+ + {/* Doctor command */}

Run the doctor command

@@ -0,0 +1,337 @@ +"use client"; + +import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from "react"; +import { useRouter } from "next/navigation"; +import { + Terminal, + Settings, + Plus, + Save, + RefreshCw, + Check, + Copy, + ExternalLink, + ArrowLeft, +} from "lucide-react"; +import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; +import { AlertCard, OutputPreview } from "@/components/alert-card"; +import { useVPSIP } from "@/lib/userPreferences"; +import { withCurrentSearch } from "@/lib/utils"; +import { + SimplerGuide, + GuideSection, + GuideExplain, + GuideTip, +} from "@/components/simpler-guide"; +import { useWizardAnalytics } from "@/lib/hooks/useWizardAnalytics"; + +export default function WindowsTerminalSetupPage() { + const router = useRouter(); + const [vpsIP, , vpsIPLoaded] = useVPSIP(); + const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); + const ready = vpsIPLoaded; + + // Analytics tracking for this wizard step + useWizardAnalytics({ + step: "windows_terminal_setup", + stepNumber: 0, // Bonus/optional step + stepTitle: "Windows Terminal Setup", + }); + + // Redirect if no VPS IP (after hydration) + useEffect(() => { + if (!ready) return; + if (vpsIP === null) { + router.push(withCurrentSearch("/wizard/create-vps")); + } + }, [ready, vpsIP, router]); + + const handleBack = useCallback(() => { + router.back(); + }, [router]); + + const displayIP = vpsIP || "YOUR_VPS_IP"; + const sshCommandLine = `ssh -i %USERPROFILE%\\.ssh\\acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@${displayIP}`; + + const handleCopy = useCallback(async () => { + try { + await navigator.clipboard.writeText(sshCommandLine); + setCopied(true); + setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); + } catch { + // Fallback + const textarea = document.createElement("textarea"); + textarea.value = sshCommandLine; + textarea.style.position = "fixed"; + textarea.style.opacity = "0"; + document.body.appendChild(textarea); + textarea.select(); + document.execCommand("copy"); + document.body.removeChild(textarea); + setCopied(true); + setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); + } + }, [sshCommandLine]); + + if (!ready || !vpsIP) { + return ( +
+ +
+ ); + } + + return ( +
+ {/* Header */} +
+
+
+ +
+
+

+ Windows Terminal: One-Click VPS Access +

+

+ ~3 min (optional but very helpful) +

+
+
+

+ Set up a custom profile in Windows Terminal so you can connect to your VPS with a single click. +

+
+ + {/* Why this is helpful */} + +
+

+ Instead of opening PowerShell and typing your SSH command every time, you can: +

+
    +
  • Click a tab in Windows Terminal to instantly connect to your VPS
    • +
  • Give it a custom name like "My VPS" or "ACFS Server"
    • +
  • Optionally set it as your default profile
    • +
+
+ + + {/* Step by Step */} +
+

+ + Step-by-Step Setup +

+ + {/* Step 1 */} +
+
+
+ 1 +
+

Open Windows Terminal Settings

+
+

+ Open Windows Terminal, then press{" "} + Ctrl + {" + "} + , + {" "}(comma) to open Settings. +

+

+ Or click the dropdown arrow (▼) next to the tab bar and select "Settings". +

+
+ + {/* Step 2 */} +
+
+
+ 2 +
+

Add a New Profile

+
+

+ In the left sidebar, scroll down and click{" "} + + Add a new profile + +

+

+ Then click "New empty profile". +

+
+ + {/* Step 3 */} +
+
+
+ 3 +
+

Configure the Profile

+
+
+
+

Name:

+ + My VPS + +

+ (or whatever name you prefer, like "ACFS Server" or "Ubuntu VPS") +

+
+
+

Command line:

+
+ + {sshCommandLine} + + +
+

+ This is your personalized SSH command with your VPS IP ({displayIP}). +

+
+
+

Starting directory (optional):

+ + %USERPROFILE% + +
+
+

Icon (optional):

+

+ You can pick any icon. The "penguin" emoji (🐧) or a cloud (☁️) work nicely for a Linux server. +

+
+
+
+ + {/* Step 4 */} +
+
+
+ 4 +
+

Save and Test

+
+

+ Click{" "} + + Save + + {" "}at the bottom of the page. +

+

+ Now click the dropdown arrow (▼) next to your tabs — you should see your new "My VPS" profile! + Click it to connect. +

+
+
+ + {/* What you'll see */} + +
+

Connecting to ubuntu@{displayIP}...

+

Welcome to Ubuntu 25.10

+

ubuntu@vps:~$

+
+ + + {/* Optional: Make it default */} + +
+

+ If you want Windows Terminal to open directly to your VPS: +

+
    +
  1. Go to Settings → Startup
    2. +
  2. Under "Default profile", select your new "My VPS" profile
    3. +
  3. Click Save
    4. +
+

+ Now every time you open Windows Terminal, it will connect to your VPS automatically! +

+
+ + + {/* Beginner Guide */} + +
+ + Windows Terminal is Microsoft's modern terminal app. It's better than the + old Command Prompt because it supports tabs, colors, and customization. +

+ If you don't have it installed, you can get it free from the{" "} + + Microsoft Store + + + . + + + +
+
+

"Permission denied" error

+

+ Make sure your SSH key file exists at{" "} + + %USERPROFILE%\.ssh\acfs_ed25519 + + . If you used a different key name, update the command line accordingly. +

+
+
+

"Connection refused" error

+

+ Double-check that your VPS IP ({displayIP}) is correct and the server is running. +

+
+
+

"Host key verification failed"

+

+ This can happen if you rebuilt your VPS. You may need to remove the old key from{" "} + + %USERPROFILE%\.ssh\known_hosts + + . +

+
+
+ + + + You can create multiple profiles for different servers! Just repeat these steps + with different names and IP addresses. + +
+ + + {/* Back button */} +
+ +
+
+ ); +}
